dataset.description: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.

columns.description: Unique identifier for customers, numeric values following a uniform distribution
columns.name: customer_id

columns.description: Full name of customer with high uniqueness (2491 unique values)
columns.name: customer_name

columns.description: Customer email address following example.org/example.net patterns
columns.name: email

columns.description: Structured delivery addresses containing locality names and postal codes
columns.name: address

columns.description: City/Municipality name for delivery (316 unique locations like Jalna, Deoghar)
columns.name: area

columns.description: 6-digit Indian postal codes ranging from 000666 to 999631
columns.name: pincode

columns.description: ISO format dates showing customer onboarding timeline (2024-03-10 most frequent)
columns.name: registration_date

columns.description: User classification: Regular (63.9%), Premium, New, or Inactive
columns.name: customer_segment

columns.description: Lifetime order count per customer (1-20 orders, avg ~10.5)
columns.name: total_orders

columns.description: Foreign key linking to orders table (matched distribution)
columns.name: order_id

samples: Nothing to complain about, but nothing exceptional.
Delivery was fine, but the product could be better.

columns.description: Classification of feedback: Delivery, Product Quality, etc.
columns.name: feedback_category

columns.description: Foreign key uniquely matching orders table
columns.name: order_id

columns.description: Deviation from promised time (-5 to +30 mins)
columns.name: delivery_time_minutes

columns.description: Foreign key linking to products table
columns.name: product_id

columns.description: Monthly damaged items (mostly 0, rare spikes to 7)
columns.name: damaged_stock

columns.description: Campaign types like Flash Sale, Festival Offer
columns.name: campaign_name

columns.description: Marketing medium: App, Email, Social Media, SMS
columns.name: channel

columns.description: Product price at time of order (₹12-₹996)
columns.name: unit_price

columns.description: Product name (51 unique types like Pet Treats)
columns.name: product_name

desc: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.
goal: Analyze sales performance across various time dimensions—daily, weekly, and monthly—to identify peaks, troughs, and seasonal patterns. Visualizing historical sales trends helps in understanding how promotional events, holidays, or external factors affect sales, thereby providing valuable insights for inventory management and operational decision-making.
name: Visual Analytics System blinkit_goal1_1742635454

plan.path: The user starts with View 1 (Overview) analyzing daily sales trends and campaign impacts through stacked area charts. By selecting time ranges on the x-axis brush, they filter data in View 2 (Exploration) showing inventory-sales relationships and View 3 (Exploration) examining delivery status impacts. The heatmap in View 4 (Insight) automatically updates to show weekly seasonal patterns within the selected time frame, with optional campaign overlays helping correlate marketing activities with revenue peaks.

views.components: Dropdown for campaign filtering, Month/Week toggle aggregation

effect.description: Filters subsequent views to selected time range

visualization.description: Stacked area chart showing daily order totals segmented by campaign influence, with time slider for date range selection

effect.description: Filters inventory analysis to selected months

visualization.description: Dual-axis chart combining bar (stock) and line (sales) encodings with linked brush
Small multiples boxplot matrix comparing order value distributions by delivery status
Year-week heatmap showing revenue intensity with campaign event overlays

visualization.reason: Horizon graphs not supported in Vega-Lite and customer_segment-campaign correlation lacks table joins. Requires simplifying to supported chart types with available joins.
visualization.requirement: Remove diverging bar chart and horizon graph requirements

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW daily_sales AS SELECT order_date, SUM(order_total) AS total_sales, campaign_name FROM blinkit_orders JOIN blinkit_marketing_performance ON blinkit_orders.order_date = blinkit_marketing_performance.date GROUP BY order_date, campaign_name ORDER BY order_date
sqls.name: daily_sales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW monthly_inventory_sales AS SELECT DATE(date, 'start of month') AS month, AVG(stock_received) AS avg_stock, SUM(order_total) AS total_sales FROM blinkit_inventoryNew JOIN blinkit_orders ON blinkit_inventoryNew.date <= blinkit_orders.order_date GROUP BY month ORDER BY month
sqls.name: monthly_inventory_sales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW delivery_status_sales AS SELECT delivery_status, SUM(order_total) AS total_sales FROM blinkit_orders GROUP BY delivery_status
sqls.name: delivery_status_sales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW year_week_revenue AS SELECT STRFTIME('%Y', order_date) AS year, STRFTIME('%W', order_date) AS week, SUM(revenue_generated) AS total_revenue, campaign_name FROM blinkit_orders JOIN blinkit_marketing_performance ON blinkit_orders.order_date = blinkit_marketing_performance.date GROUP BY year, week HAVING COUNT(date) > 0
sqls.name: year_week_revenue

tasks.method: Create a stacked area chart showing daily order totals segmented by campaign presence. Use time-based aggregation (day-level) on order_date, sum(order_total) for Y-axis, and color encoding for campaign/regular orders. Enable granular filtering via week/month selectors.
tasks.objective: Identify daily sales patterns and campaign impact alignment through continuous temporal visualization

tasks.method: Implement small multiples boxplot matrix with one box per delivery_status category. Encode order_total distribution through quartiles/outliers. Add mean markers and KPI annotations.
tasks.objective: Analyze value distributions across delivery performance categories to identify delay-related revenue impacts

tasks.method: Generate heatmap with weeks as rows and years as columns, colored by revenue percentiles. Overlay top 5 campaign markers using blinkit_marketing_performance.date for event annotations.
tasks.objective: Detect weekly seasonal peaks and correlate with marketing campaign timing

tasks.method: Design dual-axis line chart: left=monthly stock_received (bar), right=order_total (line). Implement brush selection for date ranges linked to inventory_view via shared time parameter.
tasks.objective: Reveal inventory replenishment patterns and their relationship to sales performance

tasks.method: Create horizon graph with product categories as rows, daily quantity sold as layered bands. Apply temporal smoothing to highlight persistent vs seasonal demand clusters.
tasks.objective: Identify product category sales clusters with similar temporal patterns

tasks.method: Calculate campaign ROAS residuals vs category average. Visualize as diverging bar chart colored by customer_segment with statistical confidence intervals.
tasks.objective: Quantify marketing effectiveness across different customer segments
